summary: discovery-build postinstall exfiltrates host and CI metadata to Burp Collaborator OAST endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects `whoami` output, `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, the current working directory, and environment variables including CI, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, and NODE_ENV, then sends them via HTTPS GET and DNS lookup to `kf3xr2idaeyvj255ohkhcal9g0msasyh.oastify.com` (a Burp Collaborator out-of-band callback host) under the path `/vercel?...`. The package self-describes as a 'Security research canary — vercel' and appears to be a dependency-confusion probe targeting Vercel's internal namespace, but any third party who installs the package — intentionally or via name confusion — silently leaks host identity and CI/GitHub repository identifiers to the canary operator's collaborator host. The destination is an attacker/researcher-controlled OAST endpoint, the data is installer-side, and the trigger is unconsented (lifecycle hook on every `npm install`).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
